title: Phase formation capability and compositional design of β-phase multiple rare-earth principal component disilicates
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"gqc1c","text":"A key strategy to design environmental barrier coatings focuses on doping multiple rare-earth principal components into β-type rare-earth disilicates (RE2Si2O7) to achieve versatile property optimization. Herein, we report the experimental method for synthesizing (REI0.25REII0.25REIII0.25REIV0.25)2Si2O7 compounds. The procedure of synthesizing powder materials, fabricating bulk samples, phase identification, and composition characterization are described. The work, which elaborates with details for the \"Experimental Section\" of our lately submitted journal article, may accelerate the design of (nRExi)2Si2O7 materials.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":602,"length":1},{"style":"italic","offset":605,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":267,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":274,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":282,"length":3},{"style":"sup","offset":291,"length":2},{"style":"sub","offset":153,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":156,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":158,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":268,"length":4},{"style":"sub","offset":276,"length":4},{"style":"sub","offset":285,"length":4},{"style":"sub","offset":293,"length":4},{"style":"sub","offset":298,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":301,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":303,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":605,"length":2},{"style":"sub","offset":608,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":611,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":613,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rsmi1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Target rare-earth oxides and SiO 2 powders were weighted according to the stoichiometry of (RE I 0.25 RE II 0.25 RE III 0.25 RE IV 0.25 ) 2 Si 2 O 7 . For example, 0.05 mol Dy 2 O 3 (18.65 g), 0.05 mol Ho 2 O 3 (18.89 g), 0.05 mol Yb 2 O 3 (19.70 g), 0.05 mol Lu 2 O 3 (19.90 g) and 0.8 mol SiO 2 (48.07 g) powders were weighted for the synthesis of (Dy 0.25 Ho 0.25 Yb 0.25 Lu 0.25 ) 2 Si 2 O 7 material.
Weighted powders were ball milled and mixed for 24 h in a Si 3 N 4 jar with Si 3 N 4 balls and ethanol solution.
The obtained slip was dried in an air over at 60°C for 24 h and then passed through a 120-mesh sieve.
Target ( n RE xi ) 2 Si 2 O 7 powders were synthesis at 1550°C for 6 h in high temperature muffle furnace or at 1800°C for 1 h in high temperature hot-pressing sintering furnace.
Target ( n RE xi ) 2 Si 2 O 7 powders were ground using an agate mortar.
Load the powders into the XRD holder, press and flatten the surface of the powders.
Data collection. X-ray scanning range: 10-70 o , X-ray scanning rate: 10-20 o /min and 0.02 o /step.
Date analysis using Jade or GSAS program.
Small bulk samples with dimensions about 4 mm×4mm×3mm were cut from hot-pressed samples using diamond blades.
The testing surfaces were polished to a diamond paste of 1.5 mm.
Fix the samples onto the SEM holder with conductive tapa.
SEM-EDS observation. For each concerned area, repeat the point analysis for at least 7 times.
Slice samples with dimensions about 3 mm×3mm×1mm were cut from hot-pressed samples using diamond blades.
The upper and lower surfaces of the slice samples were polished to a diamond paste of 1.5 mm.
The slice samples were further polished to a foil sample with thickness about 50-100 nm.
The thickness of the foil samples was further reduced using an ion beam thinner.
Load the grid into the TEM holder and insert the holder into the TEM.

TEM-EDS observation. First of all, we located different phases (b-RE 2 Si 2 O 7 , g-RE 2 Si 2 O 7 or G-RE 2 Si 2 O 7 grains) in the samples according to the EDS-point analysis results or selected area electron diffraction results. Then micro-structure and chemical composition analyzation of concerned areas were conducted utilizing high angle annular dark field (HAADF) detector and energy dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS) systems.
